skill.name: agent-manager-skill
skill.description: Manage multiple local CLI agents via tmux sessions (start/stop/monitor/assign) with cron-friendly scheduling.
skill.summary: Manage multiple local CLI agents via tmux sessions with start/stop/monitoring and cron scheduling.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Security audit complete. All 9 static findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation with markdown code examples - no actual shell execution, no suspicious network activity, and no cryptographic code. The backticks flagged are markdown formatting, and the URL is a legitimate GitHub repo reference.

content.value_statement: This skill enables Claude to manage multiple local CLI agents running in parallel tmux sessions, providing start/stop/monitor capabilities and cron-friendly scheduling for automated workflows.
actual_capabilities: Start and stop multiple CLI agents running in separate tmux sessions
Monitor agent output in real-time with log tailing
Assign tasks to specific agents via stdin
Schedule recurring agent work using cron syntax
List all running agents and their status
Run diagnostics to verify agent health
limitations: Requires tmux and python3 to be installed on the system
Agents must be pre-configured in an agents/ directory
Only supports local CLI agents (no remote/cloud agents)
Does not provide built-in authentication or access control

use_cases.title: DevOps Workflow Automation
use_cases.description: Run multiple background maintenance scripts in parallel, monitor their output, and schedule recurring tasks using cron.

use_cases.title: Parallel AI Task Execution
use_cases.description: Launch multiple Claude or other CLI agents simultaneously for different subtasks, then aggregate results.

use_cases.title: Background Job Management
use_cases.description: Start long-running processes in tmux sessions that persist beyond terminal sessions, with easy monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: List All Agents
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agent-manager-skill to list all currently running agents and their status.

prompt_templates.title: Start Specific Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Start agent EMP_0001 using the agent-manager-skill and show me the output.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor Agent Logs
prompt_templates.prompt: Monitor the logs of agent EMP_0002 in real-time using the agent-manager-skill.

prompt_templates.title: Assign Task to Agent
prompt_templates.prompt:
Assign the following task to agent EMP_0003 using the agent-manager-skill:

Follow teams/fractalmind-ai-maintenance.md Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Send work to a specific agent

best_practices: Configure agents in the agents/ directory before attempting to start them
Use the doctor command first to verify all prerequisites are installed
Monitor agents during initial runs to ensure they behave as expected
Use descriptive agent IDs (like EMP_0001) for easy identification
anti_patterns: Starting agents without first running the doctor check
Assigning tasks to agents that are not properly configured
Not monitoring agent output during critical operations
Running too many agents simultaneously without resource monitoring

faq.question: What prerequisites does this skill require?
faq.answer: This skill requires tmux and python3 to be installed on your system. Run 'python3 agent-manager/scripts/main.py doctor' to verify installation.

faq.question: How do I configure new agents?
faq.answer: Place agent configuration files in the agents/ directory. Refer to the repository examples for the required format and structure.

faq.question: Can I run this skill remotely over SSH?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as tmux is installed on the remote system. The skill manages local tmux sessions and does not require a GUI.

faq.question: How do I stop a running agent?
faq.answer: Use the stop command with the agent ID: 'python3 agent-manager/scripts/main.py stop EMP_0001'

faq.question: Is this skill secure?
faq.answer: The skill runs local CLI commands only. Review agent configurations before running untrusted code. No network access is required by default.

faq.question: Can I schedule agents to run on a cron schedule?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports cron-friendly scheduling. Configure scheduled tasks in the agents/ directory with cron expressions.
